title: Veterinary drugs: Establishment licence and associated fees list - Canada.ca
content:
## Establishment Licence (Drugs for veterinary use only) and associated fees
Fee as of April 1, 2025
Fee as of April 1, 2026
Fabrication - sterile dosage form
Fabrication - non-sterile dosage form
Building outside Canada (each)
If  an establishment deals with both human and veterinary drugs, then the human fee  applies.
The  following are fees associated with drug establishment licences but were not  part of the recently revised fees under the Cost Recovery Renewal Initiative.  The updated fee amounts shown below reflect the annual fee adjustment required  by the fee-setting authority governing each fee line.
Fee as of April 1, 2025
Fee as of April 1, 2026
Dealer's Licence (for Veterinary Use Only)
Fees for the examination of dealer's licence application (first year)
Dealer's Licence (for Veterinary Use Only)
Fees for the examination of dealer's licence application
If an  establishment deals with both human and veterinary controlled substances and /  or narcotics, then the human fee applies.
Fee as of April 1, 2025
Fee as of April 1, 2026
Certificate of a pharmaceutical product (CPP)
Fee  for each certificate of a pharmaceutical product Note: There are also applicable  taxes in force (PST & HST) based on the applicant's current address  (province).
Fee  for each certificate of a pharmaceutical product
For more information and how to pay the CPP fee: Guidance  document on the application for a certificate of a pharmaceutical product and  good manufacturing practice certificate (GUI-0024) .
Relevant  guidance documents and forms:
For Drug Establishment Licences:
- Fees for Veterinary  Drugs
For Dealer's Licences
- Controlled  Substances
